title: Nolaa Tarkona

{{Quote|You are trespassers - intruders, spies. Worse of all, you are Human. Humans have always tried to destroy what alien species have built. This is my private sanctuary, a place of freedom for all species. Still you have crept in and contaminated this place with your presence...|Nolaa Tarkona confronting [[Lowbacca]]'s four Human Jedi friends at her fortress on Ryloth in [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]]|<ref name="YJK:JB">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Jedi Bounty]]''</ref>}}
'''Nolaa Tarkona''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] who was the leader of the [[Diversity Alliance]]. Her teeth were filed sharp and one [[Lekku/Legends|lek]] was missing, but she had an ocular implant in its place, allowing her to see behind herself.

==Biography==
===Rise to Power===
{{Quote|You don't need to convince us, Esteemed Tarkona. We know our cause is just. The weight of human domination has crushed the galaxy for too long. We will follow you wherever the fight may take us.|[[Kambrea]]|<ref name="YJK:DOG">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Delusions of Grandeur]]''</ref>}}
Nolaa Tarkona was born on Ryloth during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. As with many other aliens, she was subject to the [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] policies of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], fueling her [[Anti-Humanism|hatred]] of [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. The Empire had also enslaved many Twi'leks and her half-sister [[Oola/Legends|Oola]] had died as a slave in [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure's]] palace when he fed her to his pet [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]].<ref name="Young Jedi Knights: Shards of Alderaan" />

Not wanting to suffer the same fate as her half-sister, an enslaved Nolaa rose up against her masters and started a rebellion, which ultimately succeeded in overthrowing the [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] government. Tarkona then became the first female Twi'lek leader in galactic history. Any Twi'lek that didn't follow Tarkana's ideals were killed, exiled or sent to work in the [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]] mines.<ref name="TEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref><ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

Soon afterward, Nolaa started the Diversity Alliance (using Ryloth's ryll profits to finance herself) and spread her message of alien unity to many species across the galaxy. She held many rallies on several alien worlds where she whipped up support for the Alliance. Many aliens joined her including [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]], [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaughts]], [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]], [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] and [[Chironian]]s. The Diversity Alliance seemed like a peaceful aliens-first movement on the outside but Nolaa created the Diversity Alliance as a way for all nonhumans to band together against a common enemy: Humans.<ref name="YJK:DOG" />

===The Emperor's Plague===
{{Quote|YES, I do have a plan. And I don't think the humans will enjoy it very much.|Nolaa Tarkona|<ref name="YJK:DOG" />}}
In [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], an alien scavenger named [[Fonterrat]] discovered the [[Emperor's plague storehouse]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. Hidden within it was a supply of deadly diseases created by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Evir Derricote]] almost two decades before. Though the Galactic Empire had been defeated, this plague warehouse still remained with its supply of deadly diseases. Knowing of Nolaa's hatred of Humans, Fonterrat offered to sell her a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] module containing the storehouse's location, though he refused to deal directly with her, suggesting that she send a neutral party to meet him on [[Kuar]].<ref name="YJK:DA">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Diversity Alliance]]''</ref><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

When negotiating, he also provided her with two samples of the virus, one of which she used to seal his payment. (She used the other on the last of the [[Four unidentified Imperial Royal Guards|Imperial Royal Guards]] as a demonstration for [[Rullak]], [[Corrsk (Diversity Alliance)|Corrsk]] and [[Kambrea]].)<ref name="YJK:DOG" /> The neutral party turned out to be a wealthy Human merchant and shipping tycoon named [[Bornan Thul]]. In exchange for the navicomputer module that contained the coordinates for the asteroid, Bornan paid with a time-locked case filled with [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="YJK:DA" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

Bornan was then supposed to deliver it to Tarkona at a trade conference on [[Shumavar/Legends|Shumavar]] but he found out about what lay within the plague warehouse and went into hiding. Enraged, Nolaa hired numerous [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to locate Thul and bring back the navicomputer.<ref name="YJK:DA" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />Among those bounty hunters was [[Ailyn Vel]], disguised as her father [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].<ref name="THOTM">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref>

===The Jedi Intervene===
[[File:Nolaa Tarkona2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Nolaa Tarkona preaching her message of alien unity to the Chromans on [[Chroma Zed]].]]
Later, Bornan's son [[Raynar Thul]] (who attended [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Praxeum]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]) went to find his father with the help of his classmates [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], [[Tenel Ka Djo]] and the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Lowbacca]]. They searched his last known locations stretching from ancient ruined worlds including [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] to the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] foundry world of [[Mechis III/Legends|Mechis III]].<ref name="YJK:DOG" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

Fonterrat also went to the all-[[Human/Legends|Human]] colony on [[Gammalin]] where he unwittingly unleashed the plague which wiped out the population. Before the plague had taken its effect, Fonterrat was imprisoned by the colonists. Since the plague had wiped out every one on the colony other than Fonterrat, he died in his cell of starvation since there was no one to take care of him.<ref name="YJK:JB" /> Meanwhile, Nolaa Tarkona continued her crusade to whip up support among the alien species of the galaxy by visiting the [[Chroman (species)|Chromans]] of [[Chroma Zed]] where she recalled Imperial oppression.<ref name="YJK:DA" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

===Hidden Secrets Exposed===
{{Quote|This vial contains more destructive power than the Death Star—than even the Sun Crusher. Selective destruction.|Nolaa Tarkona|<ref name="YJK:DOG" />}}
Later, Lowbacca was recruited into the Alliance by the female Wookiee [[Raabakyysh]] and traveled to their headquarters of Ryloth to learn more about them. Meanwhile, the Human [[Zekk]]—a friend of the Jedi—rescued Bornan from bounty hunters and learned the reason he had disappeared and the top-secret Imperial plague warehouse. Bornan also revealed that Boba Fett (actually Ailyn Vel) had sliced into his ship's navicomputer and stolen the coordinates of the plague warehouse. Ailyn Vel then presented this information to Nolaa, who wished to unleash a Human-specific plague upon the galaxy.<ref name="YJK:JB" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

Feeling that Lowbacca only knew the public face of the Diversity Alliance and not the hidden anti-Human sentiment, Lowie's Jedi companions traveled to Ryloth only to get arrested by the local authorities and put to work in the dangerous [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]] mines of Ryloth. However, Lowbacca soon learned what had befallen of his Human companions through Raabakyysh.<ref name="YJK:JB" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

The enraged Wookiee turned against the Diversity Alliance and helped them escape the harsh terrain of the planet and zealous Diversity Alliance troops. The Jedi also discovered that the Diversity Alliance was keeping Humans as slaves in its ryll mines and stockpiling weapons for its war against Humans.<ref name="YJK:JB" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" /> They were also rescued by Zekk, Luke Skywalker and [[Lusa]] (a Chironian who had left the Diversity Alliance over the death of her [[Talz/Legends|Talz]] friend and disillusionment over executing an operation).<ref name="YJK:DOG" />The Jedi then fled offworld to the galactic capital of Coruscant.<ref name="YJK:JB" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

===Her Last Stand===
{{Quote|Such sentiments are for fools. In every revolution some must sacrifice themselves to overthrow the tyrants and save the rest.|Nolaa Tarkona to [[Raabakyysh|Raaba]] following the Alliance's [[Battle of the Emperor's Plague Storehouse|defeat]]|<ref name="YJK:TEP">''[[Young Jedi Knights: The Emperor's Plague]]''</ref>}}
This incident attracted galaxy-wide publicity and a special session was held at the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] to deal with it. There, before thousands of [[Senator/Legends|Senators]] and representatives, Luke requested that an inspection team be sent to Ryloth to investigate reports of Diversity Alliance aggression.<ref name="YJK:TEP" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" /> When word of this reached Nolaa Tarkona, she ordered one of her trusted friends, the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Kambrea]], to entertain the inspection team and cover up the evidence of Alliance aggression. She also prepared a fleet to go the plague warehouse.<ref name="YJK:TEP" />

Meanwhile on the Jedi/New Republic side, after recording an encrypted message to his wife [[Aryn Dro Thul]] explaining their plan, Bornan, along with Raynar, Zekk, Jacen and Jaina Solo, Tenel Ka, and Lowbacca gathered up some explosives and went to the warehouse with the intention of destroying it. They had almost finished wiring when Diversity Alliance ships showed up. In the ensuing confrontation with Tarkona in the warehouse, some of the plague canisters were broken open, including Human and Twi'lek specific virus. Bornan sacrificed himself so the plague would not spread, and told Raynar to get himself and his friends off the asteroid.<ref name="YJK:TEP" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

Meanwhile in space, a [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic fleet]] under [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] defeated the Diversity Alliance fleet. On Ryloth, [[Battle of Ryloth (Diversity Alliance Crisis)|fighting]] took place when the New Republic inspection team discovered stockpiles of weapons. However, the fighting was stopped by the Twi'lek [[Kur (Twi'lek)|Kur]] who convinced the Alliance soldiers that they had been misguided and that both Humans and Aliens should work together.<ref name="YJK:TEP" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />

With the Diversity Alliance facing defeat, Nolaa and Raaba fled to an uncharted world in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] on their transport ''[[Rising Star]]'' where Nolaa died from a slow-acting disease which ravaged her body by wiping out her immune systems and causing her to sweat profusely. She had tried to persuade Raaba to take her to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] so that she could infect her enemies, but failed because the Wookiee had become disenchanted of the Alliance's ideals by then. This ended the Diversity Alliance, which collapsed with the death of Nolaa.<ref name="YJK:TEP" /><ref name="TEC" /><ref name="TNEC" />
